# CLAUDE.md ‚Äî Ticklr Android

## ‚öÝÔ∏è Bugs to Fix (Next Session)

### Icon PNG quality ‚Äî regenerate from SVG
**Issue:** The fallback mipmap PNG icons (mdpi/hdpi/xhdpi/xxhdpi/xxxhdpi) were generated using a Python PIL script and don't look as sharp as the adaptive icon SVG. They should be regenerated by rendering `drawable/ic_launcher_foreground.xml` as an SVG at each density.
**What to do:**
1. Install `cairosvg`: `pip install cairosvg`
2. Convert the SVG pathData from `ic_launcher_foreground.xml` into a standalone SVG file
3. Render it at each size: mdpi=48, hdpi=72, xhdpi=96, xxhdpi=144, xxxhdpi=192
4. Composite onto Navy `#0A1628` background circle for both `ic_launcher.png` and `ic_launcher_round.png`
5. Place in the corresponding `mipmap-{density}/` folders
**Reference:** iOS icon proportions in `ios/Sources/SIT/App/LaunchScreenView.swift` ‚Äî `PulseLogoMark` and `PulseWaveShape` structs



## Architecture ‚Äî MVVM + Repository
- **UI**: Jetpack Compose (no XML layouts)
- **State**: ViewModel + StateFlow
- **Persistence**: Room (version 2) + TypeConverters for List<String>
- **DI**: Hilt
- **Navigation**: Navigation Compose (NavGraph.kt)
- **Background work**: WorkManager (TickleWorker)
- **Min SDK**: 26 (Android 8.0) ¬∑ **Target SDK**: 35

## Project Structure

```
app/src/main/java/com/xaymaca/sit/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ SITApp.kt                       # @HiltAndroidApp, PREFS_NAME, KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE
```

## What's Complete ‚úÖ ‚Äî Full Android Feature Set

- Room database ‚Äî all 5 entities, 4 DAOs, StringListConverter, version 2
- Hilt DI ‚Äî DatabaseModule providing all repositories
- Compose theme ‚Äî Navy/Cobalt/Amber Pulse identity, dark-first Material3
- Full NavGraph ‚Äî 5-tab bottom nav + all nested routes with proper back stack
- `NetworkListScreen` ‚Äî searchable contact list
- `ContactDetailScreen` ‚Äî full detail view with tickle shortcut
- `AddContactScreen` ‚Äî create + edit (reused via contactId param)
- `GroupListScreen` + `GroupDetailScreen`
- `TickleListScreen` ‚Äî Due/Upcoming/Snoozed sections
- `TickleEditScreen` ‚Äî create/edit with contact/group picker, frequency, date, note
- `TickleScheduler` ‚Äî nextDueDate logic + WorkManager scheduling
- `TickleWorker` ‚Äî WorkManager Worker for background notifications
- `ComposeScreen` + `ComposeViewModel` ‚Äî multi-select + SMS send
- `SmsService` ‚Äî SmsManager direct send + Intent fallback
- `ImportScreen` ‚Äî LinkedIn CSV + contacts import
- `ContactImportService` ‚Äî ContactsContract import
- `LinkedInCSVParser` ‚Äî mirrors iOS implementation
- `SettingsScreen` + `SettingsViewModel` ‚Äî includes debug "Load Test Contacts" button
- `SeedDataService` ‚Äî DEBUG only, loads `test_contacts.csv` from assets
- `LaunchScreen` ‚Äî Pulse identity splash
- `OnboardingScreen`
- Unit tests ‚Äî LinkedInCSVParser, StringListConverter, TickleScheduler, ScreenRoute, ContactRepository (all passing)
- Build artifacts present ‚Äî app has been compiled and built successfully
- Screenshot prep ‚Äî `./gradlew screenshotPrep` sets 9:41, full signal, 100% battery, no notifications
- Screenshot teardown ‚Äî `./gradlew screenshotTeardown` restores normal status bar

## Play Store Submission Status

- ‚úÖ Signed release AAB built successfully
- ‚úÖ Keystore created at `~/Documents/ticklr-release.keystore` (alias: `ticklr`)
- ‚úÖ Signing config wired into `build.gradle.kts`
- ‚úÖ Google Play Developer account enrolled
- ‚è≥ Blocked: Google requires physical Android device to verify developer account
- ‚è≥ Once device verified: upload AAB to Play Console, complete store listing, submit

### When you find the Android device:
1. Complete Google Play developer account verification on the physical device
2. Go to play.google.com/console ‚Üí Create app ‚Üí Ticklr
3. Upload `android/app/release/app-release.aab`
4. Fill store listing from `docs/app-store-listing.md`
5. Short description (80 chars): "Privacy-first contact manager with tickle reminders"
6. Complete Data safety section ‚Äî declare no data collected
7. Set price to Free ‚Üí Submit for review (3‚Äì7 days)

### Keystore reminder
The keystore at `~/Documents/ticklr-release.keystore` is critical ‚Äî back it up to 1Password or encrypted cloud storage. Losing it means you can never update the app on Play Store.


- **App signing** ‚Äî create release keystore, configure in `build.gradle`
- **Play Store listing** ‚Äî screenshots using Pixel 7 Pro AVD + `./gradlew screenshotPrep`
- **Privacy policy URL** ‚Äî already live at `xaymaca.com/sit/privacy` ‚úÖ
- **App icon** ‚Äî ‚úÖ Already done ‚Äî adaptive icon with Pulse identity

### Android ‚Äî Nice to Have
- **SmsManager direct send UX** ‚Äî surface the "send directly vs open Messages" preference in Settings
- **Search on ComposeScreen** ‚Äî parity with iOS (filter contacts while selecting recipients)

## Key Notes

- `SITApp.PREFS_NAME` + `SITApp.KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE` ‚Äî SharedPreferences keys for onboarding state
- Room DB is version 2 ‚Äî any schema changes need a migration
- `ContactGroupCrossRef` handles the many-to-many Contact ‚Üî Group relationship
- `StringListConverter` serializes `List<String>` for phone numbers, emails, tags
- Android can send SMS silently via `SmsManager` with SEND_SMS permission ‚Äî iOS cannot
- WorkManager handles tickle notifications ‚Äî persists across reboots
- `AddContactScreen` doubles as edit screen via optional `contactId` parameter

## Pulse Brand in Compose

```kotlin
val Navy   = Color(0xFF0A1628)   // backgrounds
val Cobalt = Color(0xFF2563EB)   // primary actions
val Amber  = Color(0xFFF5C842)   // accent, tickle due state
```

## Permissions (AndroidManifest)
- `READ_CONTACTS` ‚Äî contacts import
- `SEND_SMS` ‚Äî direct SMS (runtime request, graceful fallback to Intent)
- `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` ‚Äî tickle reminders (Android 13+)

## Build & Run
Open `android/` in Android Studio (Hedgehog or newer).
Run on API 26+ emulator or physical Android device.
SMS direct-send requires physical device with active SIM.

## Sensitive Files ‚Äî Never Commit
`keystore.jks`, `*.keystore`, `release.properties`, `google-services.json`,
any file containing signing passwords or API keys.
